Content_Sketch: The signature sentence lands cold: You won't miss it. Not in the way you think.
Illustration_Note: White text on black: the sentence. Clean typography. No stickman.

Content_Sketch: Someone said this to you once. You believed it. Now you say it to others.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT sitting at tea stall, phone on table, calm posture. Scar hidden under shirt. Two cups.

Content_Sketch: The tea stall. Two cups. A low voice. This is where it starts - and where it started for you.
Illustration_Note: Wide shot of village tea stall. SUBJECT across from empty chair. Morning light. Worn table.

Content_Sketch: Debt compounds. The field yields nothing. Your child sits on the floor. Your body is the only thing left with value.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT in village. Dry field behind. CHILD sitting on dirt floor. Worn clothes. Bare feet.

Content_Sketch: Someone sits across from you at the tea stall. Same thin build. Older. They lift their shirt - scar. They say the sentence. Calm voice.
Illustration_Note: Tea stall. CHAR_RECRUITER_ORIGIN across from SUBJECT. Two cups. Shirt lifted showing scar. SUBJECT listening.

Content_Sketch: You say no. Immediately. Three days later you come back. No one called you. The sentence is still in your head. Intact.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT walking back to tea stall alone. Same table. Same cups. Nobody waiting - but SUBJECT sits down.

Content_Sketch: Overnight bus to the city. Last hours with your body still complete. You go in whole. You wake up lighter. Money less than promised. The scar is real.
Illustration_Note: Split: left - SUBJECT on bus looking out window at night. Right - SUBJECT lying on cheap hotel mattress, bandage on torso, envelope on bedside table.

Content_Sketch: Your body cannot do heavy labor anymore. The scar aches every day. The field is still dry. The debt is still there.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT trying to lift something heavy, wincing, hand on side where scar is. Village background. Same poverty.

Content_Sketch: The broker comes back. Not to buy anything. To teach you the sentence. A phone. One contact. A territory.
Illustration_Note: CHAR_BROKER handing cheap phone to SUBJECT at roadside. Brief exchange. Broker walks away without looking back.

Content_Sketch: You practice the sentence alone. You adjust the words until they sound natural. You never say sell. You never say organ. You say help. Opportunity. Just this once.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT alone in room, mouth moving silently, rehearsing. LOC_ALONE. Dim light. Repetition implied by posture.

Content_Sketch: First time saying it to someone real. The neighbor. You lift your shirt. Show the scar. They look at it. They go quiet. You say nothing else. You wait.
Illustration_Note: Tea stall. SUBJECT across from CHAR_TARGET_NEIGHBOR. SUBJECT lifting shirt showing scar. Neighbor staring. Two cups. Silence.

Content_Sketch: You put them on the bus. They look back at you one last time. You say nothing.
Illustration_Note: Bus stop at dusk. CHAR_TARGET_BUS looking back through window. SUBJECT standing still on roadside. Bus pulling away.

Content_Sketch: You say less. They say yes faster.
Illustration_Note: White text on dark background. Short. Cold. Typography only.

Content_Sketch: You recruit someone younger than you were when you sold. They say no - exactly like you said no. You use the exact sentence that was used on you.
Illustration_Note: Tea stall. SUBJECT across from CHAR_TARGET_YOUNGER. Younger person's posture mirrors SUBJECT's posture from BEAT_02_02. Same framing. Two cups.

Content_Sketch: Broker raises the quota. You already know which doors to knock on.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT walking through village at dawn. Passing houses. Knowing which ones. Phone in hand. Purposeful stride.

Content_Sketch: First time you say the sentence without thinking. You don't remember when you changed the words. You don't remember the original anymore.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT at tea stall, mouth moving, but eyes distant. The rehearsal is gone. It is reflex now.

Content_Sketch: You realize you are better at this than the man who recruited you. You keep talking. You don't hear it as a sentence anymore.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT mid-conversation with unseen target. Calm posture. Still hands. No tension in body. The skill is complete.

Content_Sketch: Someone says no. You nod. You wait. They come back. Everyone comes back.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT standing outside a house. Door closed. SUBJECT turning away calmly. Same house - door opens days later. SUBJECT returning.

Content_Sketch: You catch yourself using the sentence pattern with your family. Not recruitment. Just to get what you want.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT at home with CHAR_FAMILY. Domestic scene. SUBJECT's mouth open mid-sentence. Same calm tone. Family listening.

Content_Sketch: The person you recruited starts recruiting others. You hear the sentence from someone you never spoke to. It sounds right.
Illustration_Note: Village scene. CHAR_STRANGER talking to another figure at a tea stall. SUBJECT passing by, overhearing. Same sentence. Different voice. Same table. Same cups.

Content_Sketch: The sentence does not need you anymore.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT standing still, watching the tea stall from distance. Two figures. Two cups. The pattern continues without SUBJECT.

Content_Sketch: You won't miss it. Not in the way you think. You don't need to believe anymore.
Illustration_Note: Same typography as HOOK. Same sentence. But now the viewer understands what it means.

Content_Sketch: They still believe.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT's face. Dot eyes. Unreadable. Hold. Black.
